name: Smoked Stuffed Artichoke
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the tops and leaves of the artichokes using scissors, cutting them so that the stalks are level with the base (remove about one-third from the top and trim the tips of the remaining leaves to eliminate "spikes").
2. Place the artichokes in a kettle of boiling salted water and simmer for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a leaf can be easily pulled out. Drain, refresh under cold running water, and let stand upside down until cool.
3. To make the dressing: Sauté green onions in 2 tablespoons of olive oil until just tender. Add mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes; then add bread crumbs and cheese. Let the mixture stand until cool, stirring in white wine, lemon juice, and remaining oil. Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper, then stir in ham.
4. Prepare each artichoke by removing some of the center leaves to reach the choke (cut off about one-third from the top). Scrape out all the hairy choke using a teaspoon.
5. Fill each artichoke with the dressing and top with parsley. Wrap each artichoke in foil, leaving the top open.
6. Smoke the wrapped artichokes for 1 to 1-1/2 hours on the smoker grid.
